sections.paragraph:
- Original: συγχέω

- Transliteration: Sugcheo

- Phonetic: soong-kheh'-o

- Definition:

1. to pour together, commingle

2. to disturb the mind of one, to stir up to tumult or outbreak

3. to confound or bewilder

- Origin: from G4862 and cheo (to pour) or its alternate

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: Or συγχύνω sugchunō soong-khoo'-no From G4862 and χέω cheō (to pour) or its alternate; to commingle promiscuously that is (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder to perplex (the mind): - confound confuse stir up be in an uproar.
